Container.Name: ForgejoRunner
Container.Overview:
Forgejo Runner executes CI/CD workflows for Forgejo Actions, the built-in continuous integration system in Forgejo (a self-hosted Git forge).
This template uses host Docker socket mode — the runner dispatches workflow jobs as sibling containers on your Unraid Docker daemon. No Docker-in-Docker or privileged mode needed.
SETUP: Before starting the container you must register the runner with your Forgejo instance. See the support thread for step-by-step instructions.
1. Install this template but leave it stopped. 2. Open the Unraid terminal and run the registration command (see support thread). 3. Start the container — the runner will connect and begin picking up jobs.
Toggle "Show more settings" for runner tuning options like capacity, labels, and cache configuration.
Container.Requires: A running Forgejo instance with Actions enabled. You must register the runner before first start.

Config.Name: Data Path
Config.Description: Persistent storage for runner registration (.runner file), config, and cache. Contains the authentication token after registration — do not delete.

Config.Name: Docker Socket
Config.Description: Host Docker socket. Allows the runner to create sibling containers for workflow jobs. Required for Docker-based job execution.

Config.Name: FORGEJO_URL
Config.Description: URL of your Forgejo instance (e.g. https://git.example.com). Used during registration.

Config.Name: FORGEJO_TOKEN
Config.Description: Registration token from your Forgejo instance. Get it from Site Administration → Actions → Runners → Create New Runner. Can be reused for multiple runners.

Config.Name: RUNNER_NAME
Config.Description: Display name for this runner in the Forgejo UI.

Config.Name: RUNNER_LABELS
Config.Description: Comma-separated list of labels in format label:docker://image:tag. These determine which workflow jobs this runner can pick up. Default covers common Node and Ubuntu workflows.

Config.Name: DOCKER_HOST
Config.Description: Docker daemon endpoint. Default uses the mounted host socket. Change to tcp://host:port if using a remote Docker daemon or Docker-in-Docker.

Config.Name: RUNNER_CAPACITY
Config.Description: Number of concurrent workflow jobs this runner can execute. Increase if your Unraid server has resources to spare.

Config.Name: PUID
Config.Description: User ID the runner process runs as. Must have read/write access to the data path and Docker socket.

Config.Name: PGID
Config.Description: Group ID the runner process runs as. Set to the docker group GID if needed for socket access.
